course_title: Counselling Approaches and Mental Health Practice

learning_outcomes.description: Have an increased appreciation for the complexity of the human condition and of the neurological and psychological processes involved in human functioning.
Understand the nature trauma conditions and the potential impact that traumatic life events have on the self.
Appreciate the effectiveness of psychotherapy for a wide range of mental health conditions.
Conceptualise and develop comprehensive case formulations based on at least two evidenced-based theories of psychotherapy.
Apply a selected psychotherapy approach to the process of psychotherapy.
Apply a range of focused psychological strategies to case presentations.
Demonstrate critical and reflective reasoning to examine approaches to assessment, treatment planning and psychotherapy.

assessments.task_description: This will be an online timed task primarily focused on the topic of the Australian Health System, especially the Mental Health System.

assessments.due_date:
9/11/2026 - 13/11/2026

This assessment is presented during the first week of examinations at the end of the semester.
assessments.task_description:
Assessment 2 - Clinical Viva

This is a hurdle task.

Weighting 40%

Time: Approx. 20mins

Task:

Part A: Case Formulation (15 Mins)

Your task is to participate in a clinical viva (in person) wherein a written case study will be provided at the start of the assessment and you are to write a one-page case formulation within 15 minutes. You may choose to address the case using either ACT or PIT.

Part B: Oral Presentation (5 mins):

In Part B you are to present a 5-minute explanation of your case formulation based on the chosen theoretical approach and how you intend to address the issues therapeutically. The oral should consider:

  • ACT or PIT formulation.

o  Key case issues.

  • Primary therapeutic focus and actions
  • Treatment rationale.

Live component focuses entirely on:

•   Clarification.

•   Clinical reasoning.

•   Theory application.

Part C: Oral Defence (5 mins)

In Part C the assessor will ask 3–4 questions about your rationale and application.

Example questions

·      Why is ACT/PIT preferable to another approach for this case?

·      What would be your primary treatment target?

·      What would you do if the client did not engage?

·      What intervention would be prioritised in Session 2?

Hurdle Task:

This assessment task must be passed to pass the course. It will be graded on a six-point scale – 40%, 33%, 27%, 20%, 15%, 0%. If the task is not passed, students will be given the opportunity to resit the assessment if a mark of 15% is awarded. A passing grade for a resit will be limited to a grade of 20%.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Part A: 3000 words

In this assignment your task is to write a reflective essay on how you would apply Psychodynamic Interpersonal Therapy (PIT) to a case study provided in class. In reflecting on your application of PIT you should consider the following:  

  • A brief description of the case and your case formulation (400 words).
  • An outline of the key principles and processes of PIT (1000 words)
  • An outline of the first four counselling sessions that you would likely conduct and any challenges associated with providing counselling support for the client (800 words)
  • Your essay discussion should include reference to relevant literature to support your argument.

Part B: 800 words

In addition to a reflection on the application of PIT, you are also required to consider how the case may have been conceptualised differently if ACT was the principal theoretical approach being applied. What might be the strengths and limitations of ACT applied to this case compared to PIT?
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assignment can be submitted via Turnitin. Instructions will be provided in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
